title: activate_subscription parses expires_at from invoice string; re-serializes to same format — parse+format round trip is fragile
description: payment_watcher.rs activate_subscription: parses invoice.expires_at (a YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS string) into NaiveDateTime, calls .and_utc(), then .max(now + fallback_days) — then passes the resulting DateTime<Utc> to activate_subscription_atomic which re-formats it to YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. The expires_at field is also re-used in the SUBSCRIPTION_ACTIVE notification as expires_at.format('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'). This parse-then-reformat round trip is done to support the .max() comparison, which is reasonable. But the InvoiceRow.expires_at field is a String, not a DateTime — which forces every consumer to re-parse it. The field type should be DateTime<Utc> or a newtype; storing it as a formatted string and parsing at every use site is the root of this awkwardness.
